This plant is native to the Americas and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Thelypteris is a part of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes over 10,000 species of ferns. intro: Thelypteris concinna, also known as the Silver Spleenwort or Silver Shield Fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris concinna include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Thelypteris concinna is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, tonics, and creams to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
